_schema._doc: Card copy banks for the three beats (BORN · DIED). Pure data — enrich this file with more clauses in ANY session and the generator (src/model/copy.js) picks them up with no code change. Selection is a deterministic hash of the life (shareable lives reproduce), with an optional sliding-window de-dup at display time (src/model/copy.js `recent`). Aim: large banks per cell so play feels fresh at volume (the stateless pick can't beat the birthday bound — big banks + de-dup do).

tokens.{phrase}: the chosen origin phrase (BORN frame only)
tokens.{originClass}: noun for the class they were born into ('poverty', 'working class', …)

guards._doc: A clause may be a plain string, or an object {text, when}. `when` filters the clause out when it doesn't fit the life. Combine freely.
guards.when.minClimb: integer — require at least N class-bands climbed (use with {classesUp})
guards.when.originLow: true — only for lives born lower/working class (use with {originClass})

born.self: On an origin phrase: true means the phrase ALREADY names the place ({flag} {country}), so it pairs with bornFrames.self (no trailing 'in {country}').

keys.origin: by class band: lower | working | middle | upper | elite (upper-mid collapses to upper)
keys.diedOpeners: by tier mood: warm (BLESSED+) | neutral (MID) | grim (ROUGH/FAIL)
keys.diedTails: by tier band: top (LEGENDARY/MYTHIC) | good (BLESSED/EPIC) | mid (MID) | low (ROUGH/FAIL)
keys.fatal: death caused by an event (cause folds in)
keys.cutShort: died young of natural causes: infant (age ≤ 1) | child (age 2–17)

self: Born {phrase} — the hand they were dealt.

lower: with the odds stacked against them
working: to people who worked for every cent

elite.text: with the world handed to them

top: old money to the very last day
the kind of life they build statues for
richer than most see in ten lifetimes
a life that rewrote what was possible

top.text: and the climb itself became the legend

good.text: a good few rungs up from {their} start
better than the start, by a real measure
further than anyone expected of {their} start

good: more than enough, and no regrets
mid: an ordinary life, start to finish
a quiet life that asked for little

low.text: the {originClass} that raised {them}, to the very end

low: no further along than {their} first day
the same hard road, start to finish
the world never gave an inch

fatal: {cause}, and that was the end — at just {age}.

infant: Gone as an infant — a life barely begun.
Lost in infancy, before it could start.
child: Gone at just {age} — a life barely begun.
Taken at {age}, before it could start.
